description: Assign drivers to fleet vehicles for defined periods, maintain a full assignment history, and enforce constraints preventing double-assignment and unauthorised transfers.

actors.name: Fleet Manager
actors.description: Creates, modifies, and ends driver-vehicle assignments

actors.name: Driver
actors.description: Is assigned to the vehicle; may view their current and historical assignments

actors.name: System
actors.description: Enforces assignment constraints and auto-ends assignments when a new one is created

values.name: draft
values.description: Assignment prepared but not yet activated

values.name: active
values.description: Assignment is in force; driver is currently assigned to the vehicle

values.name: ended
values.description: Assignment has been completed or terminated

transitions.description: Assignment confirmed and takes effect from start_date
Assignment ended — reassignment or driver departure

no_double_permanent_assignment.description: A vehicle cannot have two active permanent assignments simultaneously

no_overlapping_assignments.description: A vehicle cannot have more than one active assignment per driver type at the same time (one permanent + temporary assignments are permitted if temporal ranges do not overlap)

end_date_after_start.description: End date must be after start date when provided

permanent_ends_by_new.description: A permanent assignment may only be ended by creating a new assignment for the same vehicle (which auto-ends the previous one)

end_reason_required.description: Ending an assignment requires an end_reason

driver_must_be_active.description: Driver must have Active status in the driver registry to receive a new assignment

start_date_not_past.description: Start date cannot be in the past for new assignments (warn if creating a backdated record)

decommission_ends_all.description: When a vehicle is decommissioned, all active assignments are automatically ended

given: vehicle exists and is in active status
driver exists and has Active status
no conflicting active assignment exists for the vehicle and date range
end_date is after start_date if provided
assignment_created.result: Driver is assigned to the vehicle; vehicle master record reflects the new driver

given: an active permanent assignment already exists for this vehicle
assignment_conflict_rejected.result: New assignment is rejected; fleet manager must end the existing assignment first

given: an active permanent assignment exists for the vehicle
new assignment is being created as permanent
fleet manager explicitly confirms the transition
previous_permanent_auto_ended.result: Previous permanent assignment is auto-ended and the new one is activated

then.description: Clear assigned driver from vehicle master if no replacement assigned

assignment_ended.result: Assignment is ended; vehicle master's assigned driver is cleared

given: a vehicle or driver filter is provided
history_query.result: Full assignment history for the specified vehicle or driver is returned including ended and cancelled records

errors.message: This vehicle already has an active permanent assignment. End the existing assignment before creating a new one.
The selected driver is not active. Only active drivers can be assigned to vehicles.
The assignment end date must be after the start date.

events.name: assignment.created
events.description: A driver has been assigned to a fleet vehicle

events.name: assignment.ended
events.description: A driver-vehicle assignment has been terminated

events.name: assignment.history_queried
events.description: The assignment history for a vehicle or driver has been retrieved

related.reason: Vehicle master stores the currently assigned driver and must be updated on each assignment change
Costs can be attributed to the driver assigned during a given period via assignment history

goals.description: Assign drivers to fleet vehicles for defined periods, maintain a full assignment history, and enforce constraints preventing double-assignment and unauthorised transfers.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: workflow steps must complete correctly before proceeding
